aboutPreview.headline: An invitation, not an argument.
aboutPreview.body: The Straight Path is a small, independent effort to share Islam in a calm, reader-first voice — pastoral rather than polemical, for seekers of every background.

learn.title: Learn About Islam
learn.description: A curated set of articles introducing the core ideas, character, and practices of Islam — written for readers of any background.

articlesPage.arabicComingSoon: This article is available in English for now. An Arabic translation is in preparation, in shā' Allāh.

quranPage.headline: Read the word of God, preserved for over 1400 years.
quranPage.body: We recommend Quran.com — a trusted, free resource that offers the Qur'ān in many languages with a simple and beautiful interface.
quranPage.aboutBody1: The Qur'ān is the final revelation from God (Allāh), revealed through the angel Gabriel to the Prophet Muḥammad ﷺ over 23 years. It has been preserved — word for word, in its original Arabic — through both written transmission and continuous memorization across generations.
quranPage.aboutBody2: It is the central religious text of Islam — a book of guidance, mercy, and wisdom. Muslims believe its preservation is divinely safeguarded, and its message is a continuation of the one sent to earlier prophets: worship God alone, and do good.

resourcesPage.title: Useful External Links
resourcesPage.description: Trusted resources for further study — chosen for their accuracy, accessibility, and tone.

faqPage.title: Ask Questions
faqPage.description: Plain answers to common questions about Islam — and an open door for the rest.

items.q: What is Islam, in one sentence?
items.a: Islam is the voluntary submission of the heart to the one God (Allāh), expressed in worship, ethics, and daily life — a tradition Muslims trace through all the prophets from Noah to Jesus to Muḥammad ﷺ.

items.q: Do Muslims worship a different God than Christians and Jews?
items.a: No. Muslims worship the same One God who spoke to Abraham, Moses, and Jesus. "Allāh" is simply the Arabic word for God. Arabic-speaking Christians and Jews also call God "Allāh."

items.q: Do I have to learn Arabic to be Muslim?
items.a: No. A Muslim can be of any language. Arabic is the language of the Qur'ān, so many Muslims learn some Arabic for prayer and reading scripture, but full translations are widely available.

items.q: What are the five pillars of Islam?
items.a: Testimony of faith (shahāda), daily prayer (ṣalāh), charity (zakāh), fasting in Ramadan (ṣawm), and pilgrimage to Mecca once in a lifetime if able (ḥajj).

You sincerely declare the testimony of faith: "There is no god but Allāh, and Muḥammad is the messenger of Allāh." That is the beginning of the path; what follows is a lifetime of learning and growth.

items.q: I have more questions. Who can I ask?
items.a: Please use the contact form. We read every message and respond with care — there is no pressure, just conversation.

aboutPage.title: About The Straight Path
aboutPage.intro: The Straight Path is an independent, volunteer effort to share Islam in a calm, reader-first voice. Our aim is not to argue but to invite — to make the essentials of Islam clear, accessible, and honest, for anyone curious enough to open the page.

principles.label: Pastoral, not polemical.
principles.body: We write as if speaking to a thoughtful stranger over coffee.

principles.label: Source everything.
principles.body: Qur'anic verses and authenticated hadith, cited and graded.

principles.label: Non-sectarian.
principles.body: We teach core Islam and leave sectarian debates at the door.

principles.label: Plain language.
principles.body: Arabic terms are defined on first use and transliterated consistently.

aboutPage.fundingBody: This project is volunteer-run. Its infrastructure is intentionally minimal — a static React site on Firebase Hosting, cached aggressively. If you'd like to contribute time or translation help, please reach out.

contactPage.title: Contact
contactPage.description: Have a question about Islam? A correction? A thought? We read every message.
contactPage.thankYou: Thank you — your message has been received.
contactPage.error: Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment.

notFound.seoDescription: The page you're looking for isn't here. Try the home page, the articles index, the Qur'an, or send us a message.
notFound.title: We couldn't find that page.
notFound.description: The link may be broken, or the page may have moved. If you've wandered off the path, that's alright — let's find your way back.
notFound.contactHint: Tell us what you were looking for

about.label: About
about.hint: Who we are, why we built this

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.intro: The Straight Path is a content site. We aim to collect as little personal data as possible.

collectItems.label: Contact form submissions.
collectItems.body: When you submit the contact form we store your name, email, and message in Firebase Firestore so we can reply.

collectItems.label: Basic analytics.
collectItems.body: Aggregate, anonymous page-view data via Firebase Analytics. No cross-site tracking.

collectItems.label: Technical logs.
collectItems.body: Firebase Hosting records standard request logs (IP, user-agent) for short periods.

whatWeDont: We do not sell or share data with advertisers.
We do not use tracking cookies beyond what's required for the site to work.
privacyPage.rightsBody: You may request deletion of any message you've sent us by emailing the address on the contact page.

termsPage.title: Terms of Use
termsPage.intro: The Straight Path is provided free of charge, on an as-is basis, for educational purposes. By using the site you agree to the following:

items.accuracy: Content is offered in good faith; we strive for accuracy but make no guarantees.
items.licensePrefix: Articles may be quoted with attribution. The source code is available under the MIT License; original text content is under
items.abuse: Please don't abuse the contact form or attempt to disrupt the service for others.
